title: feat: add daily app health checks with stale app removal
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: The hive tends its garden
gist.blurb: We've taught the hive to tend its own garden. Broken apps now get gently pruned after a week of downtime to keep the showcase fresh.
gist.summary: Implemented an automated daily health check system that monitors community apps and proposes removal for those unresponsive for 7 consecutive days.
gist.impact: The app showcase will remain reliable and free of broken links, ensuring a better experience for visitors exploring the ecosystem.
gist.visual_concept: Gardening metaphor. The pollinations.ai bee acts as a gardener in a digital greenhouse. The plants represent apps; healthy ones are vibrant lime green, while the bee inspects a 'withered' or glitchy one with a clipboard/checklist. The mood is constructive maintenance, not destruction.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the bee mascot wearing a tiny gardener's hat and holding a clipboard, inspecting a row of pixelated code-plants. Most plants are glowing lime green (#ecf874), but one looks slightly glitchy and grey. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, wholesome Stardew Valley vibes. Text in pixel font: 'GARDENING'. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites.
